content: Use Mulch for durable project knowledge. Before changing focused files, query/scope-load related records; after discovering a reusable convention, architectural decision, failure, or pattern, record it with evidence instead of leaving it only in chat.

Use Seeds as the active tracker for roadmap and implementation work. Claim work with `sd_update(... status="in_progress")`, close completed issues with `sd_close`, and create follow-up issues instead of expanding ISSUES.md as the primary tracker. Keep .seeds/ committed via `sd sync` when ready.

title: Retired ISSUES.md in favor of Seeds
rationale: Actionable follow-up work from the legacy roadmap was migrated into Seeds issues, so ISSUES.md was removed to avoid split-brain planning. CLAUDE.md is kept because Seeds/Mulch agent onboarding writes useful integration context there even if Claude is not the primary interface.

content: For multi-step feature planning, create one Seeds epic for context plus narrow numbered tracer-bullet task issues. Do not make the first task depend on the epic, otherwise the open epic blocks ready work; use explicit dependencies only between implementation steps.

title: README favors concepts over file/module inventories
rationale: Because the project is in heavy development, README documentation should avoid project-structure and module-reference inventories that rot quickly. Keep README focused on status, core concepts, Orchestrator Libraries, dashboard capabilities, terminal commands, tools, and development usage.
